title: choerospondias axillaris
intro: Choerospondias axillaris, also known as the Indian olive, is a species of flowering plant in the family Anacardiaceae. It is a deciduous tree native to the Himalayas and is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Ayurvedic medicine. The plant has been found to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help in treating various health issues such as fever, digestive problems, and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of Choerospondias axillaris include a decoction of the bark, which is used to treat various ailments, and a paste made from the leaves, which is applied topically to soothe skin irritations.
